summary: The docker-compose.yml implementation is correct and complete. All 8 acceptance criteria pass: both services use the specified image versions, both ports are bound exclusively to 127.0.0.1, named volumes are declared at the top level and mounted at the correct container paths, and no application containers are present. The file is minimal and well-aligned with the task specification.

acceptanceCriteriaResults.criterion: docker compose config --quiet passes (valid YAML + Compose schema)
Named volume mongodb_data declared and mounted at /data/db
Named volume redis_data declared and mounted at /data
No application containers (only mongodb and redis services)
acceptanceCriteriaResults.evidence: File contains exactly two services: mongodb, redis

suggestions.suggestion: Add a top-level 'name' field (e.g. name: simple-sender-infra) to give the Compose project an explicit stable name. Without it, Docker derives the project name from the directory name ('infra'), which is a generic name that could collide with other projects on a developer's machine using the same directory name.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents accidental cross-project volume or network collisions when multiple projects use a generic 'infra/' directory layout. Makes docker compose ps, docker compose down, and volume listings unambiguous.

suggestions.suggestion: Consider adding a restart policy (restart: unless-stopped) to both services. Currently, if the containers exit unexpectedly, they will not restart. For local dev where developers often run 'docker compose up -d' once at the start of the day and expect services to survive across machine wakes/sleeps, this reduces friction.
suggestions.benefit: Improves local developer experience by automatically recovering services after Docker Desktop restart or machine sleep without requiring manual 'docker compose up -d' again.
